The Armenian Orthodox Church is one of the five Oriental Orthodox Churches. It was officially recognized as a Church  with a Patriarach as it's head in 267 AD.
It is also noteworthy that Queen Helena was a Princess of the Armenian Royal Family & she was the mother of Emp. Constantine,  who captured Turkey & named it's Capital as Constantinople &  declared it as the Capital of the Eastern Roman Empire.

comments.author: @thomaszachariah4756
comments.text:
With Apologies, would like to add some more information.
St. Thomas, an apostle of Jesus reached the South West Coast of Southern India in 52AD.
His main objective was  to speak about Jesus to the small Jewish communities spread over the West Coast of the present Indian State of Kerala.
St. Thomas built 8 Churches from 52 to 62 AD, in 8 different places on the Kerala Coast.
This Church was formally approved as the Malankara( place between Hills & Sea shore) Orthodox Syrian  Church, in 231 AD,  by the Council of Bishops who were then in Jerusalem & and were trying to formalize the structure of priests, Bishops etc.
As per my information, the Armenian Orthodox Church was officially approved as an Orhtodox Church in 267 AD.
I also know that Queen Helena, mother Emp. Constantine was a Princess from Armenia.
I have been to Jerusalem, and have visited the Holy Sepulchre Church, where a Capel in the name of Queen Helena exists.

comments.author: @omnidirectional2339
comments.text: While Armenia was the first to adopt Christianity as a state, Macedonia holds the distinction of being the first place in Europe where the Gospel was preached and accepted.The Meeting: Around 50 AD, the Apostle Paul had a vision of a "man of Macedonia" pleading for help. He traveled to Macedonia.The First Convert: Instead of a man, Paul first found a group of women praying by a river outside the city. Among them was Lydia, a wealthy merchant who sold expensive purple cloth.The Impact: After she and her household were baptized, Lydia opened her home to Paul and his companions. This home became the first Christian church in Macedonia.

comments.author: @ManukyanAnita
comments.text:
The architecture of Armenian churches   is designed to emphasize the interior space, a sense of the nearness of God. The focus is often on the mystical, vertical, and spiritual dimension rather than external decoration. 
There is no excessive, gilded iconography, but a more restrained approach.

comments.author: @gorgtem
comments.text: I am part Armenian myself, but to say that it is the oldest country with direct continuity is a misrepresentation. One of the oldest countries with direct cultural and civilizational continuity, yes. The oldest country with direct continuity, a big resounding no. It has lost statehood altogether and was only brought back as a unified country or Republic for that matter, by the creation of the Soviet Union. Facts please. They are important.

comments.author: @keithad6485
comments.text:
I have been intrigued by Armenian history after two occurences.

When visiting Western Front battle sites in France and Belgium me and my mate visited a US WW1 war cemetery. the Caretaker, a retired US Marine officer, told us he was American Armenian, and told us about the 1915 genocide of Armenians in Turkey, then several years later when doing research for a book I am writing on Aussie soldiers in WW1, I read 1915 Intelligence Reports (original documents still held in Australian War Memorial) and came across reports of mass deaths of Armenians by Turks.  That these reports were in daily Divisional/Brigade/Regimental intelligence reports means, the reports, though about events which took place a few hundred miles away behind the Turkish frontier, were taken seriously. I have been interested in Armenian history since. The reports of the genocide are horrific. they commenced the day before Aussie troops first landed on Gallipoli in northern Turkey.

comments.author: @LamarPassmore
comments.text:
Ahmad bin Muhammad bin Sa’eed narrated from Ahmad bin Muhammad ad-Daynawari from Ali bin al-Hasan al-Kufi from Omayra bint Ouss from her grandfather al-Hussayn bin Abdurrahman from Abdullah bin Dhamra that Ka’bul Ahbar had said:

“On the Day of Resurrection the human beings will be resurrected in four categories; riders, walking people, groveling people and deaf, dumb and blind people, who do not understand, are not spoken to and not permitted to apologize. These are whom (the fire shall scorch their faces, and they therein shall be in severe affliction).” It was said to him: “O Ka’b, who are these people, whose condition will be so?” He said: “It is those, who have kept on deviation, apostasy and breaking the homage. Evil is that which their souls have sent before for them when they will meet Allah with the sin of fighting the caliph and the guardian of their prophet, their most aware jurisprudent, their most virtuous master, the bearer of the banner, the guard of the pond, the hope and the hoped one out of all this world, the knowledge that is never ignored, the straight path, from which whoever deviates will perish and fall in Fire. It is Ali, by the god of Ka’b, who is the most aware of them, the senior in peace (Islam) and the most discerned of all of them. I wonder at those, who prefer others to Ali. From among the descendants of Ali is al-Qa’im al-Mahdi, who will change the world into another one and by whom Jesus Christ ￼ will argue against the Christians of Rome and China. Al-Qa’im al-Mahdi is the most similar one to Jesus Christ ￼ in form, character, nobility and dignity. Allah will give him all what He has given to the prophets and more than that and He will prefer him to the all. Al-Qa’im, the descendant of Ali ￼, will disappear like the disappearance of Prophet Joseph ￼ and will reappear like the reappearance of Jesus the son of Mary ￼. He will reappear, after his disappearance, with the appearance of the red star, the destruction of az-Zawra’ (ar-Riy), the sinking of al-Muzawarra (Baghdad), the rising of as-Sufyani, the war of the progeny of al-Abbas against the people of Armenia and Azerbaijan, in which thousands and thousands will be killed. Each one of the warriors will seize on an adorned sword, over which black banners will flutter. It will be a war full of red death and terrible plague.”
